rulings.Case Title: MARTIN RYAN VS. 1001 CALIFORNIA STREET CONDOMINIUM HOMEOWNERS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Diane Wiley As Trustee For The Estate Of George Wileys Joinder, And Joinder, In Motion By Pacific Union International, Inc. And Joanne Stein To Motion To Bifurcate
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Aug 17, 2020 line 1. DEFENDANT DIANE WILEY's Joinder In Motion By Pacific Union International, Inc. And Joanne Stein To Motion To Bifurcate Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person (a good faith reason for personal appearance is required) for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendant's Joinder in Motion to Bifurcate is DENIED without prejudice to be brought before the trial judge if necessary. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: MARTIN RYAN VS. 1001 CALIFORNIA STREET CONDOMINIUM HOMEOWNERS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant 1001 California Street Condominium Homeowners Associations Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication, Of Plaintiffs Third Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Aug 17, 2020 line 1. DEFENDANT 1001 CALIFORNIA STREET CONDOMINIUM HOMEOWNERS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication, Of Plaintiffs Third Amended Complaint Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person (a good faith reason for personal appearance is required) for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendant's Motion for Summary Judgment. is GRANTED. Motion for Summary Adjudication is GRANTED as to the first, second, third and fourth causes of action. Moving party shifted its burden. Opposing party failed to create a triable issue of fact with competent admissible evidence. Defendant's objections to Ansel Kinney Declaration 2-12 are sustained; objection 1 is overruled. Motion for Summary Adjudication is GRANTED as to the seventh cause of action to the extent it is asserted against the moving defendants. This cause of action is either duplicative of the second cause of action or was not intended to be alleged against the moving defendants. Motion is treated as a motion for judgment on the pleadings as to the causes of actions for breach of governing documents (5th ) and breach of Davis-Sterling Act (6th ) and is granted with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege each element of this cause of action as to the failure to provide alternative heating to the unit upon termination of central heating. = (501/CFH)
